page: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed local storage directly attached via the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated mathematical and matrix operations, alongside hardware-enforced security features like Total Memory Encryption.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2201.0 and a multi-core score of 21132.0, placing it in the top tier of tested systems. Redis SET operations reach 112.74 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput peaks at 105.65 GB/sec. Large-block memory read bandwidth is strong at 196.04 GB/sec, though large-block write bandwidth is moderate at 79.24 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference prompt processing achieves 297.03 tokens/second for a 7B model and 39.06 tokens/second for a 70B model.

Given its high-density DDR5 memory, dedicated compute cores, and integrated 3.8 TB NVMe storage, the m8id.16xlarge offers a balanced resource profile that is highly cost-efficient for data-intensive workloads without requiring separate storage attachments. It is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed by the Nitro hypervisor. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier performance, achieving a Geekbench multi-core score of 21132.0 and processing 112.74 million Redis SET operations per second. It also exhibits strong memory read bandwidth of 196.04 GB/sec and 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This hardware profile is highly efficient for workloads requiring a balance of fast local storage, high-speed memory, and dedicated compute, such as database hosting, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and compute benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-performance general-purpose server combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, fast DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Includes 3800 GB of direct-attached local NVMe SSD storage
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math
Delivers top-tier performance in Redis database operations and static web serving
Optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
